workflow.critical: The workflow execution engine is governed by: ~/.nakiros/core/tasks/workflow.xml
You MUST have already loaded and processed: {installed_path}/workflow.yaml
YOU MUST ALWAYS SPEAK OUTPUT in the configured communication_language resolved from profile and workspace settings.
Generate all backlog-facing content (epic, stories, tasks, summaries) in {{document_language}}.
Keep internal artifact keys in ENGLISH for machine-readability and future automation.
Execute ALL steps in exact order; do NOT skip steps.
This workflow plans backlog structure only. Do not claim that backlog entities were created unless a later action step really does it.

step.action: Load `workspace.yaml` from the current working directory. This is the canonical workspace map.

Set {{workspace_name}}, {{workspace_slug}}, {{workspace_topology}}, {{workspace_repo_list}}, and {{pm_tool}} from `workspace.name`, `workspace.slug`, `workspace.structure`, `repos`, and `workspace.pm_tool`. If the yaml is missing, continue in single-repo mode with the current directory only.

Load profile config from {profile_config} when available (optional). Apply defaults when missing: user_name={default_user_name}, communication_language={default_communication_language}, document_language={default_document_language}.
Override {{document_language}} with `workspace.document_language`.

Load workspace-global context from files: - Read {workspace_context_root}/product.md when present. - Read {workspace_context_root}/personas.md when present. - Read {workspace_context_root}/architecture/index.md when present. - List folders under {features_root}/ as {{existing_features}}.

If the user has already run plan-feature for this feature, read {features_root}/{{feature_slug}}/feature.md as {{existing_feature_md}} — use it as the starting point for discovery instead of asking from scratch.
step.output: [PLAN-FEATURE] Étape 1/8 — Load configuration ✓ Config loaded | Workspace: {{workspace_name}} | Topology: {{workspace_topology}}

step.goal: Run PM discovery for the feature
step.critical: Load PM persona from ~/.nakiros/agents/pm.md for this discovery and slicing phase.
This is a collaborative product conversation, not a form dump. Ask one focused question at a time when clarification is needed.
When the feature is cross-repo or platform-wide, anchor the conversation in the workspace-global product and architecture context instead of drifting into one repo too early.
step.ask: What feature do you want to plan? Describe the outcome you want, who it helps, and any hard constraints already known.
step.action: Capture the initial request as {{feature_intent}}.

When the problem is still vague, ask clarifying questions such as: - What changes for the user when this feature ships? - What is explicitly out of scope for this first cut? - Are there deadlines, integrations, or legacy constraints? - What would make this feature a failure even if it ships?
step.output: [PLAN-FEATURE] Étape 2/8 — PM discovery ✓ PM framing complete | Problem, scope, and expected outcome are clear enough to slice

step.goal: Define the epic and slice the feature into stories
step.critical: Stay in PM persona. Optimize for a backlog structure that is understandable, reviewable, and implementable.
Create one epic and a small set of delivery-sized stories. Prefer 3 to 7 stories unless the user explicitly wants a broader plan.
Do NOT output epic or story content in the chat. Keep everything in memory. The only allowed chat output is the step marker at the end of this step.
step.action: Generate {{feature_title}} and {{feature_slug}} from the clarified feature intent. Keep the title short and searchable.

Slice the feature into {{stories}} where each story includes: - id (STORY-1, STORY-2, ...) - title - user outcome / description - acceptance criteria (3-5 measurable bullets) - priority (critical / high / medium / low) - story_points (estimate only if confidence is reasonable; otherwise null) - dependencies - out_of_scope_notes if needed

Challenge the slicing before moving on: - Merge stories that are artificially split. - Split stories that are too large or mix multiple outcomes. - Flag any story that is actually a technical task and move it out of story scope.
step.output: [PLAN-FEATURE] Étape 3/8 — Define epic and stories ✓ Epic and story slicing complete | Ready for technical decomposition

step.goal: Run Architect review to create technical tasks
step.critical: Load Architect persona from ~/.nakiros/agents/architect.md for this decomposition phase.
Architect reviews the PM slicing against technical reality, then adds tasks. Architect does not rewrite the product problem unless the slice is technically invalid.
Do NOT output tasks or technical decomposition in the chat. Keep everything in memory. The only allowed chat output is the step marker at the end of this step.
step.action: Use context-first behavior: - Read {workspace_context_root}/product.md when present — use it as product context. - Read {workspace_context_root}/architecture/index.md when present — use it as architecture context. - For relevant repos, read {workspace_context_root}/architecture/{repo_slug}/index.md when present before proposing technical work. - Only ask for extra clarification when code reality or architecture constraints materially change the task plan.
For each story in {{stories}}, produce: - technical_summary - architecture_notes - risks - suggested_repo_scope - tasks

Each task must be delivery-oriented and technically actionable, not a vague reminder. Every task includes: - title - description - kind (frontend / backend / infra / data / qa / design-system / integration) - depends_on (optional list) - technical_notes (optional)
When architect review exposes a wrong story cut, adjust the story boundaries before finalizing tasks and record the reason in {{architect_adjustments}}.
step.output: [PLAN-FEATURE] Étape 4/8 — Architect review ✓ Architect decomposition complete | Stories now have technically grounded tasks

step.critical: Do NOT output any artifact content in the chat during this step. Keep all generated content in memory variables only. The only allowed output is the step marker at the end of this step.
step.action: Prepare {{stories_table_rows}} for the feature.md stories table update: For each story: "| {{story.id}} | {{story.title}} | 📋 À implémenter |"
Set {{has_frontend_scope}} = true if any story task has kind=frontend, or if {{workspace_repo_list}} includes a repo identified as a frontend repo in the architecture context. Otherwise set to false.

check.action: Load {workspace_context_root}/design-system.md as {{design_system}} when present — use it to extract primary color, background, font family, and border-radius tokens for the mockup.

Build {{mockup_html_content}}: a self-contained HTML wireframe showing the main screens of this feature. - Use inline CSS only — no external stylesheets, no CDN links. - If {{design_system}} was loaded, extract and apply the primary color, background, font family, and border-radius tokens. Otherwise use a clean neutral palette (background: #f8f9fa, primary: #2563eb, text: #1e293b). - Show each screen as a distinct labelled section. Use a top navigation strip or tab bar to switch between screens conceptually (static tabs, no JS required — just visually distinct sections stacked vertically with clear headers). - Use semantic HTML (nav, main, section, form, table, button, header) with realistic placeholder content matching the feature's domain. - Include the key states defined in {{ux_md_content}}: normal state, empty state, and at least one error or loading state per screen. - Focus on layout and component placement — no decorative gradients or shadows. Keep it low-fidelity but structurally accurate. - Add a fixed top bar: "Nakiros Mockup — {{feature_title}}" | {date} | workspace: {{workspace_name}}.

step.output: [PLAN-FEATURE] Étape 5/8 — Build artifacts ✓ {{stories | count}} story file(s) prêts{{if has_frontend_scope: " | UX + maquette HTML prêts"}}
[PLAN-FEATURE] Étape 6/8 — Plan prêt ✓ — Écriture du preview…

step.critical: Write preview files directly using your native Write filesystem tool. Do NOT output file contents in chat.
step.action: Read your system prompt. Locate the [NAKIROS_SESSION] block and extract the value of conversation_id. Capture it as {{nakiros_conversation_id}}. If the block is not present or conversation_id is missing, generate a fallback identifier in the format conv_{unix_timestamp_seconds}_{6_random_alphanumeric_chars} (e.g. conv_1743200000_a3f8k2).
Define {{preview_root}} = {workspace_root_dir}/.preview/{{nakiros_conversation_id}}/features/{{feature_slug}}
step.output:
[PLAN-FEATURE] Étape 7/8 — Preview écrit ✓
📂 **{{feature_title}}** {{stories | count}} story file(s) — {{for each story: "{{story.id}}"}} {{if has_frontend_scope: "🎨 UX design + maquette HTML inclus"}}
Fichiers dans : `{{preview_root}}/`
**Que faire ?**
1. [nakiros:preview:apply] Appliquer — copier dans le context final 2. Modifier un fichier — précise lequel et ce qui doit changer 3. [nakiros:preview:discard] Annuler — supprimer les fichiers de preview

check.if: user wants to edit a file
check.action: Read the relevant preview file from `{{preview_root}}/` using your native Read tool. Apply the requested changes in place using your native Write tool. Do not regenerate unchanged sections.
check.output: ✏️ Modifié. Relis le fichier dans ton éditeur, puis clique sur Appliquer ou demande d'autres ajustements.

step.ask:
**Et maintenant ?**
1. Implémenter une story — /nak-workflow-dev-story 2. C'est bon pour l'instant

check.output: Lancez /nak-workflow-dev-story en précisant la story à implémenter.
